Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Join us to shape the future!

Teamtailor is a global Employer Branding and ATS SaaS platform used by over 12,000 companies, 200,000 users, and available in 90 countries worldwide. 🌍

Working at Teamtailor means being part of a dynamic, fast-paced tech company that values impact and responsibility. Our workplace fosters an environment where everyone can contribute meaningfully to the growth of the company. 🎉🥳

Building a diverse team with a wide range of experiences, skills, and backgrounds has always been central to Teamtailor’s mission, and it remains a strength today. We welcome all individuals who share our vision and are committed to contributing to our collective success. Join us in helping companies and people communicate in more meaningful ways to make life-changing decisions together.👫

We are looking to recruit an Analytics Engineer to own the certified analytics layer: the curated datasets and governed metric definitions that power trusted reporting and self-serve across the business.

For this role you would sit at the intersection of data, business, and governance. Your core mission is to ensure that key metrics are defined once, controlled centrally, and consistent everywhere, while improving data quality and reducing discrepancies caused by hidden dependencies (spreadsheets, untracked transformations, and ad-hoc logic).

Ideally the person for the role will understand and have worked with Data Analytics Engineering, has experience with owning certified datasets, metric definitions, and the semantic/metrics layer governance as well as the ability to create and maintain metric definitions in close partnership with RevOps/GTM/Finance, and ensure they remain consistent across downstream consumption.

Key Responsibilities

Data modeling:


Design, build, and maintain analytics marts in BigQuery using dbt, with clear grains, conformed dimensions, and scalable patterns.
Design and own the “gold” layer used for company KPIs
Ensure curated datasets are documented, discoverable, and easy to use for downstream consumers.


Metric Definitions And Governance


Lead metric definition work through structured collaboration with RevOps/GTM/Finance:
clarify business intent, edge cases, inclusions/exclusions
document definitions and assumptions
Implement governance so metrics are defined in one place and changes are controlled and propagated
Establish lightweight processes for approving and evolving metrics

Data Quality And Discrepancy Management


Improve trust in data by implementing strong quality practices
Drive investigation and resolution of discrepancies
Reduce reliance on spreadsheet logic and undocumented transformations by migrating business-critical logic into tested, version-controlled assets.


We Are Looking For Someone With


3+ years in an Analytics Engineer / Data Analyst / similar role
Strong SQL and data modeling skills
Proven track record of defining metrics with stakeholders and turning ambiguity into clear, durable definitions
Strong ownership mindset around:
data quality and reliability
documentation and clarity
governance that enables (not blocks) self-serve
Comfortable working cross-functionally and influencing without authority

Nice To Haves


Experience working in B2B SaaS and/or sales-led GTM analytics (pipeline, forecasting, churn/NRR, attribution tradeoffs)
Experience with semantic/metrics layers (regardless of technology) and metric governance frameworks
Familiarity with BI tooling and how downstream consumers use modeled data.


If this sounds like you, please click apply for this job or reach out to our TA team

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Photowall

At Photowall, we create custom-made wallpapers and wall murals for customers all over the world. We’re a growing e-commerce company, with a current presence in over 30 markets, and we’re continuing to expand - both in new markets and with new products.

We work in a fast-paced environment where things move quickly, ideas are tested, and everyone is encouraged to take ownership. If you want to develop, try new things, and be part of a team that gets things done - you’ll fit right in.

About The Role

We’re looking for an Analytics Engineer to help build and scale the analytics foundation that powers decision-making across Growth, Marketing, Product, and Commercial teams.

This is a highly cross-functional role where you’ll sit at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and business strategy. You’ll own and evolve our analytics infrastructure - from data pipelines and warehouse models to dashboards, experimentation analysis, and marketing measurement.

You’ll work closely with stakeholders across the business to turn complex data into clear insights and actionable recommendations. At the same time, you’ll help ensure our data ecosystem is scalable, reliable, and built for future growth.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys both the technical side of analytics engineering and the commercial impact of helping teams make smarter decisions faster.

Main Responsibilities

Data Infrastructure & Pipelines


Own and maintain BigQuery data pipelines, including scheduling, monitoring, and data quality validation
Integrate data from multiple sources - including GA4, Klaviyo, marketing platforms, and backend systems - into a centralized data warehouse
Build and maintain scalable data models serving both marketing and product use cases
Ensure data reliability through proactive monitoring, alerting, and clear documentation


Analytics & Dashboards


Design, build, and maintain dashboards for Growth, Marketing and Product teams
Translate business questions into clear analyses with actionable recommendations
Own ad-hoc analysis for senior stakeholders, including executive-level reporting
Support experimentation workflows and A/B test analysis


Event Tracking & Digital Analytics


Implement and maintain event tracking through Google Tag Manager (GTM) and GA4
Partner with Product and Engineering to define and QA tracking instrumentation across the web funnel
Own funnel analysis and ensure tracking accuracy across key user journeys


First-Party Data & Marketing Measurement


Build and maintain the data infrastructure that feeds CRM (Klaviyo) and paid media channels - including audience exports, customer match, and consent-compliant data flows
Support marketing measurement including attribution modelling, channel performance analysis, and media efficiency reporting
Partner with Digital Marketing on campaign analysis and ROAS reporting


Requirements


4-6 years of experience in data analytics, analytics engineering, or a similar hybrid role within e-commerce or digital products
Strong SQL and BigQuery skills; experience with dbt, Dataform, or similar transformation tools
Proficiency with Looker (or a similar BI tool) and Funnel
Proven experience building and maintaining data pipelines across multiple sources
Strong communication skills - comfortable explaining technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Fluent in English


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4, GTM, and digital analytics instrumentation
Familiarity with CRM data flows (Klaviyo or similar) and paid media data structures (Meta, Google Ads)


Why Photowall?

At Photowall, you’ll be part of a collaborative and fast-moving environment where data plays a central role in how we grow and make decisions. You’ll work closely with teams across Growth, Marketing, Product, and E-commerce, with real impact on how we scale across international markets.

As part of the Network of Design (NOD), we continue to evolve how creativity, technology, and data come together to shape the future of Photowall.

We value ownership, curiosity, and continuous improvement - and give you the opportunity to shape and evolve our analytics foundation while working with modern tools and large-scale e-commerce data.

Apply today and help us build the analytics capabilities that power the future of Photowall.

At Photowall, you’ll be part of a collaborative and fast-moving environment where data plays a central role in how we grow and make decisions. You’ll work closely with teams across Growth, Marketing, Product, and E-commerce, with real impact on how we scale across international markets.

As part of the Network of Design (NOD), we continue to evolve how creativity, technology, and data come together to shape the future of Photowall.

We value ownership, curiosity, and continuous improvement - and give you the opportunity to shape and evolve our analytics foundation while working with modern tools and large-scale e-commerce data.

Apply today and help us build the analytics capabilities that power the future of Photowall.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Are you a skilled Analytics Engineer with a passion for transforming raw data into high-quality, actionable insights? Join Sweden's largest property portal to design the data solutions that power our business decisions.

As the most popular real estate portal in Sweden, Hemnet receives over 40 million visits every month from people looking for their next home. We are a data-heavy organization, and we strive to make data-informed decisions the standard for everything we do.

About The Role 🍏

As an Analytics Engineer, you will be a key player in our journey to empower the organization to become more data-driven. Sitting within the Analytics team, you will act as a vital bridge between raw data and meaningful business logic. Because this role connects technical infrastructure with business needs, strong communication is essential.

By focusing on the "back end" of business intelligence to ensure our data is clean, reliable, and well-modeled, you will play a crucial part in helping Hemnet continuously improve and become better for everyone.

What You’ll Do 💥


Build and develop data models, taking responsibility for structuring the data that forms the foundation for analysis, reporting, and future AI solutions.
Conduct analysis and ad-hoc explorations to answer complex business questions, helping stakeholders across Hemnet turn raw data into insights.
Improve our self-service capabilities, enabling more colleagues to find answers independently through robust data architecture.
Occasionally support the development of essential reports and dashboards to ensure data is visualized effectively.


Who we think you are 🤩

In order to be successful in this role we believe that you have an educational background with a strong technical focus and a few years of experience working as an Analytics Engineer, Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Developer. As a bridge between technical and business teams, you excel at tailoring your communication and insights for everyone from Sales and Business Development to the leadership group.


Strong SQL and sound judgement in data modelling.
Professional experience with dbt.
Passionate about using your data models to solve real-world business problems alongside your stakeholders.
Experience partnering across departments and strong business acumen.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Swedish.
Bonus if you have experience in:
Tableau
A semantic or metrics layer
AI-powered self service analytics
Python


I am your new manager 👋

My name is Thomas Hanusek. I joined Hemnet about four years ago. Prior to that, I drove analytics for more than 20 years across different companies. My passion at work is really about seeing how Data & analytics can drive product and business development in the best way possible.

As a leader, I am a huge fan of seeing how a team can come together and, through their different skills, create magic. My aim is to be supportive and humble, and to create an environment where you feel trusted, challenged, and empowered to grow.

A Note on Inclusion 💚

At Hemnet, we believe that diversity in gender, sexual orientation, ability, neurodiversity, cultural background and life experience makes us stronger. We welcome applicants of all identities and backgrounds.

If you meet many but not all of the requirements, we encourage you to apply. We value curiosity, a strong sense of purpose and the motivation to learn and grow, alongside your skills and experiences.

An Office-First Culture 🙏

Our office in central Stockholm is the true hub of our company culture. We are proudly an office-first team because we deeply value face-to-face collaboration and believe our best work happens when we are together. While we understand the need for occasional flexibility, we strongly prioritize in-person teamwork over remote work. Because our office is the center of our daily operations, it is essential that you are based in or near Stockholm.

Please note that we're unable to provide relocation assistance for this opportunity.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics (Maternity Cover)
Description:
THE OPPORTUNITY IN A NUTSHELL

You? A data-driven storyteller who lives to turn complex marketing signals into clear, actionable growth strategies.

Role? As our Senior Data Analyst, you’ll build the framework that defines how we measure and attribute success across the Nordics. This is a 9-month cover for Maternity

Tech stack? Snowflake, dbt, Tableau, Amplitude,

Company? Vend, home of FINN, Blocket, Tori, Oikotie, DBA & Bilbasen, where millions of people across the Nordics connect to find what they need, whether it’s a job, a home, a way to move, or a fresh start.

Location? Join us in our central and vibrant Oslo, Stockholm or Helsinki office, with the flexibility of working 3 days remote.

Why us? This is your chance to shape the measurement culture of a Nordic powerhouse. You’ll have the autonomy to build models from the ground up, influence tech roadmaps, and see your insights directly impact the engagement of millions of users.

Sounds like your cup of tea? Check out the details below!

WHO ARE YOU?


Analytically fearless with a proven track record in marketing attribution and performance reporting.
Technically savvy, comfortable auditing tracking solutions and collaborating with tech teams to ensure data integrity.
A forecasting pro who can build reliable models to predict user behavior and marketing ROI.
Experienced in SQL, Python/R, advanced attribution modeling, and deep dives into tracking architecture.
Communicative & Collaborative, able to translate "data-speak" into strategic advice for non-technical stakeholders.
Proficient in English (our working language), while knowledge of a Nordic language is a plus for understanding our local marketplaces.


WHAT’S THE JOB LIKE?

Day-to-day work & projects cooking. As our Senior Data Analyst within the User Engagement Team, your mission is to bring "the source of truth" to our marketing efforts. You aren't just running reports; you are architecting the logic behind them.


The Goal: To provide a holistic view of how marketing spend converts into long-term user engagement.
The Week: You might spend Monday refining an attribution model, Wednesday advising the engineering team on tracking implementation, and Friday presenting a performance forecast to the leadership team.
Key Projects: You will lead the creation of a brand-new Performance Reporting Framework and optimize our cross-channel attribution logic.
Stakeholders: You’ll be the bridge between Marketing leads, Product Managers, and Data Engineering.


The challenge turns into an opportunity! Currently, our tracking and attribution landscape is complex and evolving. You might find gaps in data structure or legacy processes that need a refresh. This is your playground, you have the green light to dismantle what isn't working and build a scalable, modern analytics infrastructure that you can truly call your own.

A FEW WORDS FROM THE MANAGER

Hi, I am Tarun Tolia, Head of User Engagement & Marketing Analytics. Our User Engagement Team is a dedicated group of specialists focused on keeping our millions of users coming back. We are a collaborative bunch who value curiosity over hierarchy. I believe in giving my team the autonomy to solve hard problems, and I’m looking for someone who isn't afraid to challenge the status quo with data.”

Want to get a feel for our culture and what drives us? Take a peek behind the scenes at our Career Page!

GOT YOUR ATTENTION?

Let us hear from you! Turn yourself in by 14th June 2026. We will start reviewing applications on a rolling basis, so don't wait!

QUESTIONS?

Tarun Tolia, Head of User Engagement, is happy to provide information about the daily work and answer any questions you may have about the role via email: tarun.t.tolia@vend.com. For the recruitment process, you can reach out to Clayton Don Corda at clayton.don.corda@vend.com

Title: Technical Analyst (Level 4) – Digital Analytics & CRO
Description:
Location: Stockholm area (Hybrid / Remote flexibility)

Language Requirement: Swedish required

About The Assignment

We are looking for an experienced Digital Analyst for a long-term consulting assignment within a Product Analytics team. The role is focused on developing and optimizing digital customer experiences across web and mobile applications, with a strong emphasis on e-commerce, digital marketing, and conversion optimization.

You will work within a central analytics organization collaborating closely with multiple product and development teams to enable data-driven product and business development. The assignment requires solid expertise in digital analytics, e-commerce or digital products, and the ability to independently drive analytics initiatives within a complex organization.

Responsibilities


Drive conversion rate optimization (CRO) initiatives and customer flow analysis
Own analysis of A/B tests from hypothesis definition to impact evaluation
Perform ad hoc analysis of user behavior across web and mobile applications
Identify improvement opportunities and analyze customer journeys across multiple digital platforms
Ensure data quality through validation, troubleshooting, and requirement setting
Support product teams and business stakeholders with data-driven insights and recommendations
Translate complex analytical findings into actionable business insights for both technical and non-technical audiences


Environment & Collaboration

The analytics ecosystem is primarily based on Google platforms with integrations toward BigQuery and modern cloud-based data platforms. You will collaborate closely with product management, UX/design teams, developers, and business stakeholders.

The assignment can largely be performed remotely, though occasional onsite presence in the Stockholm area may be required.

Required Skills & Experience


Minimum 5 years of experience as a Digital Analyst
Strong SQL skills
Hands-on experience with BigQuery
Experience with Google Analytics
Experience with Google Tag Manager (including server-side templates)
Experience with Data Studio / Looker Studio and data visualization
Experience with Hotjar


Meritorious Skills


Google Ads
Google Search Console
Experience with A/B testing tools such as VWO
Experience within retail media or digital advertising
Looker or other self-service analytics/reporting tools
Attribution and attribution modeling
AI-supported analytics using tools such as Gemini or similar


Personal Profile


Analytical and structured mindset
Self-driven and proactive
Strong communication and presentation skills
Comfortable working cross-functionally between technology, product, and business teams
Ability to transform data into concrete recommendations and measurable business value

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Description

Position at LeoVegas Group

About The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support P&T initiatives by analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to drive decision-making.This role is a unique opportunity to venture into 'uncharted territories,' focusing on how our products work and analyzing the performance of new features to drive our commercial and product strategy forward.

As a Data Analyst, you will be a key player in investigating customer journeys and website search performance, providing data-driven recommendations that directly impact the user experience. Working in a fast-paced environment in a team based in Malta, Sweden, Poland, and Italy, you will have the chance to build projects from scratch, revamp dashboards, and find areas for improvement within our global gaming product.

YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:


Product Analysis: Analyze website search performance and player insights to identify "loopholes" and areas for product improvement.
Stakeholder Management: Build strong, mature relationships with product owners, management, and commercial teams to communicate data-driven recommendations.
Customer Journey Investigation: Use behavioral and product analytics to deep-dive into how users interact with our platforms.
Dashboard Development: Collaborate with the team to revamp existing dashboards and create new tracking projects centered on product analytics.
Proactive Reporting: Conduct proactive analysis of new feature performance and provide actionable insights to the wider data team.
Data Unification: Help unify previously separate analysis tasks following the merger of our behavioral and commercial functions.


OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING:

Essential Skills


Professional Experience: Minimum of 2+ years of experience in a Data Analyst role, preferably within a product-focused environment.
Advanced SQL: Strong proficiency in SQL for querying and managing complex data sets.
Analytics Expertise: Proven experience in behavioral and product analytics, specifically investigating customer journeys.
Statistical Knowledge: A solid foundation in statistics to ensure the accuracy and reliability of your findings.
Communication Skills: The ability to translate complex data into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive Attitude: A self-starter mindset with the maturity to take ownership of projects and build stakeholder trust.
Python Proficiency: Experience using Python for data manipulation or advanced analysis.


NICE TO HAVES


iGaming Experience: Previous experience within the iGaming industry is considered a significant asset.


Who We Are

At the core of LeoVegas Group is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry.

We're a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices worldwide.

Benefits


Hybrid work policy
4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
Well-being allowance to support your active lifestyle
Private health insurance
Discounts across a range of retailers, gyms, bars & restaurants
We offer an employee assistance program that can provide help and guidance during challenging moments.


JOIN US!

In our pride, we empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait for things to happen; we pounce and make it happen!

Would you be a good fit for the Leo Pride - give us a roar!

**As our company working language is English, we’d like to see your CV in English, please**

Title: JUNIOR DATA ENGINEER
Description:
Att jobba som Data Engineer

Som Data Engineer blir du en viktig del av ett datateam som arbetar med att bygga moderna, molnbaserade datalösningar i Azure. Du arbetar nära erfarna data engineers, data platform engineers och analytiker och får successivt ta mer ansvar i att utveckla integrationsflöden, data pipelines och datamodeller som används i hela organisationen. Du ser till att säkerställa att data är tillgänglig, kvalitetssäkrad och lätt att använda för både analytiker och verksamheten – så att bättre beslut kan fattas snabbare.

I rollen kommer du bland annat att:


Bygga och underhålla enklare datapipelines och integrationer
Hjälpa till att utveckla och förbättra organisationens Azure-baserade dataplattform
Arbeta med data från olika källsystem och bidra till dataintegration
Stötta i modellering och strukturering av data i datalake och data warehouse
Delta i utveckling av dataprocesser i exempelvis Azure Data Factory och Databricks
Samarbeta tätt med data engineers, analytiker och verksamheten



Rollen är placerad i Stockholm.

Att vara konsult på Nexer Tech Talent

Nexer Tech Talent är specialister på att ge techtalanger den ultimata starten på arbetslivet. För denna roll kommer du att bli anställd av Nexer Tech Talent men arbeta hos vår kund.

Vi söker dig som:


Har Relevant utbildning inom systemvetenskap, data eller liknande
Grundläggande kunskaper i SQL och gärna Python
Intresse för data, integrationer och molntjänster
Förståelse för hur system och data hänger ihop
Nyfikenhet och vilja att lära dig moderna dataplattformar



Meriterande är om du har:


Erfarenhet av Azure eller liknande molnplattform (AWS/GCP)
Arbetat med data pipelines, ETL eller API:er
Kännedom om Databricks eller liknande verktyg
Erfarenhet från projekt inom data eller systemutveckling



Ansökan

Låter detta som något för dig? Vi arbetar löpande med rekryteringen, så skicka in din ansökan snarast möjligt.

Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!

Om Nexer Tech Talent

Nexer Tech Talent är en del av Nexer Group med väletablerade ramavtal, långsiktiga samarbete och några av Sveriges mest välkända och betydelsefulla kunder. Tillsammans är vi över 2500 medarbetare i 16 länder som i mer än 35 år hjälpt våra kunder ligga steget före både strategiskt, teknologiskt och kommunikativt. Läs mer om oss här.
Show more

About Us

We currently employ experienced consultants in areas such as System Development, AI, Machine Learning, Testing, Cloud, Infra DevOps, and IT Project Management.

We simply offer more than just a high salary and greater freedom; that’s just the beginning. With us, you’ll find the work environment and opportunities you deserve. Welcome to Deploja, where the grass is indeed greener!

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Join us to shape the future!

Teamtailor is a global Employer Branding and ATS SaaS platform used by over 12,000 companies, 200,000 users, and available in 90 countries worldwide. 🌍

Working at Teamtailor means being part of a dynamic, fast-paced tech company that values impact and responsibility. Our workplace fosters an environment where everyone can contribute meaningfully to the growth of the company. 🎉🥳

Building a diverse team with a wide range of experiences, skills, and backgrounds has always been central to Teamtailor’s mission, and it remains a strength today. We welcome all individuals who share our vision and are committed to contributing to our collective success. Join us in helping companies and people communicate in more meaningful ways to make life-changing decisions together.👫

We are looking to recruit an Analytics Engineer to own the certified analytics layer: the curated datasets and governed metric definitions that power trusted reporting and self-serve across the business.

For this role you would sit at the intersection of data, business, and governance. Your core mission is to ensure that key metrics are defined once, controlled centrally, and consistent everywhere, while improving data quality and reducing discrepancies caused by hidden dependencies (spreadsheets, untracked transformations, and ad-hoc logic).

Ideally the person for the role will understand and have worked with Data Analytics Engineering, has experience with owning certified datasets, metric definitions, and the semantic/metrics layer governance as well as the ability to create and maintain metric definitions in close partnership with RevOps/GTM/Finance, and ensure they remain consistent across downstream consumption.

Key Responsibilities

Data modeling:


Design, build, and maintain analytics marts in BigQuery using dbt, with clear grains, conformed dimensions, and scalable patterns.
Design and own the “gold” layer used for company KPIs
Ensure curated datasets are documented, discoverable, and easy to use for downstream consumers.


Metric Definitions And Governance


Lead metric definition work through structured collaboration with RevOps/GTM/Finance:
clarify business intent, edge cases, inclusions/exclusions
document definitions and assumptions
Implement governance so metrics are defined in one place and changes are controlled and propagated
Establish lightweight processes for approving and evolving metrics

Data Quality And Discrepancy Management


Improve trust in data by implementing strong quality practices
Drive investigation and resolution of discrepancies
Reduce reliance on spreadsheet logic and undocumented transformations by migrating business-critical logic into tested, version-controlled assets.


We Are Looking For Someone With


3+ years in an Analytics Engineer / Data Analyst / similar role
Strong SQL and data modeling skills
Proven track record of defining metrics with stakeholders and turning ambiguity into clear, durable definitions
Strong ownership mindset around:
data quality and reliability
documentation and clarity
governance that enables (not blocks) self-serve
Comfortable working cross-functionally and influencing without authority

Nice To Haves


Experience working in B2B SaaS and/or sales-led GTM analytics (pipeline, forecasting, churn/NRR, attribution tradeoffs)
Experience with semantic/metrics layers (regardless of technology) and metric governance frameworks
Familiarity with BI tooling and how downstream consumers use modeled data.


If this sounds like you, please click apply for this job or reach out to our TA team

Title: Spontanansökan (Stockholm, SE, 117 61)
Description:
Implenia Sverige AB har närmare 500 anställda och har många års erfarenhet och kompetens inom utmanande infrastrukturprojekt. Företaget tillhandahåller i dagsläget tjänster inom betongkonstruktioner, broar, tunnlar och bergrum samt väg- och anläggningsarbeten. Kontor finns i Stockholm och Göteborg.

Implenia Sverige är en del av Implenia Group, som är den ledande leverantören av byggtjänster i Schweiz. Koncernen har också en stark ställning på den tyska, österrikiska och norska infrastrukturmarknaden. Implenia Sverige grundades 2006 men har över 150 år av erfarenhet inom branschen. Med sin integrerade affärsmodell och specialister inom alla kompetensområden kan koncernen som en multinationationell leverantör av byggtjänster leverera ett ekonomiskt och kundcentrerat arbete genom hela livscykeln.

Företaget fokuserar på en hållbar balans mellan ekonomisk framgång, socialt ansvar och miljöansvar. Implenia Group har sitt huvudkontor i Dietlikon nära Zürich och har mer än 8 500 medarbetare i Europa. 2020 var omsättningen cirka 4 miljarder schweiziska francs (40 miljarder svenska kronor).

Följ gärna Implenia Sverige AB på LinkedIn och Facebook.

Skicka in en spontanansökan

Finns det inget utannonserat jobb som passar dig just nu? Då kan du lämna in en spontanansökan som vi matchar med våra behov och möjligheter. På Implenia värderar vi mångfald, vår rekryteringsprocess är därför inriktad på att främja jämställdhet och inkludering.

Vi stämmer kontinuerligt av inkomna spontanansökningar med Implenias behov och möjligheter och återkommer till dig om vi hittar ett passande jobbförslag.

Var vänlig och notera att vi tyvärr inte har möjlighet att lämna individuell återkoppling på din spontanansökan.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
We’ve signed up to an ambitious journey. Join us!

As Arrive, we guide customers and communities towards brighter futures and more livable cities, it isn’t a challenge just anyone could take on. Luckily, we have something to help us make it happen. Our people and our values. We Arrive Curious, Focused and Together. Just as our entire brand is inspired by the North Star, the shining light leading travelers to their destinations since time began, our values guide us. They help us be at our best. For our customers. For the cities and communities we serve. For ourselves. As a global team, we are transforming urban mobility. Let’s grow better, together.

The Role

Are you ready to tackle complex challenges and make a global impact? Join our Access Controlled Solutions team as a Business Analyst, where you'll play a vital role in shaping the next generation of parking solutions.

In this role, you'll work with a variety of stakeholders—from camera provider partners and distribution teams to the product team—to ensure our solutions function seamlessly. You’ll dive deep into processes, identifying improvements, creating detailed documentation, and aligning multiple internal teams to provide a smooth user journey from start to finish. You will help streamline our access control solutions, from simplifying user flows to enabling scalable, automated processes that elevate the EasyPark experience.

Our products within access controlled parking solve real-world parking challenges, enabling users to seamlessly enter and exit access-restricted areas via automatic license plate recognition and other access methods. With over 6 million vehicles opted in and millions of transactions processed monthly, we’re dedicated to creating a flawless experience for EasyPark users across the globe.

How To Make An Impact


Work cross-functionally to optimize processes, facilitating smooth product launches and performance scaling as we aim to make this solution available to users worldwide
Evaluating business processes, clarifying and anticipating requirements and uncovering areas for improvement
You will help the Product Team by figuring out the tricky business details to ensure we build products that meet business and customer needs
Analyze, evaluate, refine and document business processes and create new models that support the business
Documenting features, functionalities and overall how products and services work on both a holistic and granular level
Effectively communicating your insights and plans to cross-functional team members and management


About You

Curiosity and an analytical mindset are essential to succeed in this role. You have a strong drive to understand complex, interwoven processes and can distill them into clear, actionable documentation for diverse stakeholders. You’re comfortable navigating a variety of challenges, balancing a big-picture perspective with an eye for detail. You excel at zooming in on the finer aspects of product flows while maintaining a holistic view of the overall experience.

Your background


Experience: 1–3 years as a Business Analyst, Product Analyst, or in a related role, preferably within a technology, data, or product-focused environment
Analytical Skills: Strong analytical abilities, with a knack for breaking down complex processes, identifying bottlenecks, and proposing actionable improvements
Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, capable of translating technical details into clear, understandable documentation for non-technical stakeholders
Collaboration: A team player who thrives in cross-functional environments, with experience working alongside engineering, product, and business teams
Process-Oriented: Comfortable working within complex workflows and adept at understanding and documenting detailed processes to drive efficiency and clarity.
Language: Fluent in English, both written and verbal


This role will ideally be based in Stockholm, however, we are open to considering candidates across the UK and Europe.

Title: Placeringssamordnare
Description:
Har du idéer för framtiden och drivs du av utmaningar? I Haninge får du utvecklas tillsammans med trygga kollegor som vill göra skillnad redan idag. Just nu pågår ett av de mest ambitiösa förändringsarbetena i vår kommuns historia. Vi bygger en ny stad, cirka 20 minuter från Söder. Följ med på vår resa. 



 



Välkommen att utvecklas med oss



Haninge växer och vi söker nu en placeringssamordnare som vill bidra till att säkra rätt stöd för våra invånare.  Vi söker dig som vill ta en central roll där ditt arbete bidrar till att göra skillnad för barn, unga och vuxna med behov av bland annat både interna/externa boendelösningar och öppenvård. Hos oss får du möjlighet att arbeta i en utvecklingsinriktad miljö där du är med och bidrar till att utforma arbetssätt, processer och strukturer.



Vi söker nu en till placeringssamordnare för en tillsvidareanställning.



Ditt uppdrag



Du ansvarar för avrop från ramavtal och direktupphandling av insatser riktade till barn, unga, unga vuxna samt vuxna.



I nära samarbete med dina kollegor bidrar du i hanteringen av inkomna placeringsförfrågningar från socialsekreterare/biståndshandläggare men även platsförfrågningar och prisförhandlingar med externa leverantörer. I tjänsten ingår att hantera samt utreda avvikelser kopplade till direktupphandlade avtal. I det dagliga arbetet har du ett nära samarbete med privata utförare, stabens kvalitetsteam & avtalscontroller, myndighetsavdelning samt andra funktioner inom den egna förvaltningen. Du är en drivande kraft i förvaltningens gemensamma placeringsteam och deltar aktivt i placeringsforum.



Du arbetar löpande med direktupphandlingar och ser till att dessa sker i enlighet med lagen om offentlig upphandling (LOU). Du ansvarar för att tillsammans med socialsekreterare/biståndshandläggare följa upp direktupphandlade avtal och placeringar samt granskning av underlag kopplat till utföraren. Arbetet innefattar att förhandla platspriser, uppföljning av avtal, omförhandla avtal, granska fakturor och säkerställa att kraven och fakturaunderlag efterlevs i praktiken för samtliga externa placeringar. 



Rollen har ett förvaltningsövergripande perspektiv och i det operativa arbetet samordnas uppdraget i nära samarbete med förvaltningens avtalscontroller. I tjänsten ingår även att du vissa dagar kan komma att hantera akuta placeringsinsatser. Arbetet kan innebära resor runt om i Sverige.



Exempel på arbetsuppgifter:

Aktivt söka och matcha utförare för insatser inom IFO
Genomföra avrop och direktupphandlingar enligt LOU
Följa upp direktupphandlade avtal tillsamman med ansvarig socialsekreterare/biståndshandläggare
Förhandla priser och villkor Hantera avvikelser kopplade till utförare
Delta i placeringsforum och arbeta operativt i placeringsprocessen
Registrera handlingar och dokumentera enligt gällande rutiner 

 Det här söker vi hos dig

Socionomexamen
Erfarenhet av socialt arbete
Erfarenhet av myndighetsutövning enligt SoL, LVU och LVM samt om vårdformer inom individ- och familjeomsorgen 
Kunskap och erfarenhet av avtalshantering 
God förmåga att uttrycka sig i både tal och skrift och att du är väl förtrogen med svenska språket

Meriterande

Erfarenhet från liknande funktion
Erfarenhet av LOU
Erfarenhet av myndighetsutövning enligt LSS
Erfarenhet av Ciceron
Erfarenhet av LifeCare 

Det är viktigt för oss att du är en stabil person som kan behålla lugnet i svåra situationer. Du har också ett lösningsorienterat och empatiskt förhållningssätt och är tydlig och strukturerad i ditt arbete. Du är självgående och har förmåga att ta egna initiativ för att se till att arbetet flyter på. Du har god samarbetsförmåga och lägger stor vikt vid att ge ett gott bemötande, både internt och externt. Samtidigt har du ett tydligt fokus på kvalitet i alla delar av uppdraget och ett ansvarsfullt förhållningssätt till hur skattebetalarnas medel används och förvaltas. Vi arbetar ständigt för en god arbetsmiljö och lägger därför stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet.



Anställning kan erbjudas efter att godkänt registerutdrag från polisen erhållits. Provanställning kan komma att tillämpas. 



Det här kan vi erbjuda dig



Ett utvecklande arbete som engagerar och utmanar, tillsammans med kunniga och erfarna kolleger. Vi sitter i ljusa trevliga kontorslokaler med goda parkeringsmöjligheter och närhet till köpcentrum, restauranger, naturområde samt till Handens pendeltågstation, endast 20 minuter från Stockholm city. Vi har fri tillgång till gym i kommunhuset, friskvårdsbidrag, årsarbetstid, klämdagar samt möjlighet till semesterväxling. Haninge kommun tillämpar distansavtal och möjlighet till upp till två dagar på distans finns i den mån verksamheten tillåter.



Vill du veta mer?



Gå gärna in på vår hemsida där du kan läsa mer om verksamheten: www.haninge.se



Om du har frågor om tjänsten kan du kontakta:



Tarik Dzabirov, enhetschef tel 08-606 92 16 eller mejl tarik.dzabirov@haninge.se 



Välkommen med din ansökan! 



I den här rekryteringen har vi redan gjort våra val av annonskanaler och samarbetspartners. Vi undanber oss vänligt men bestämt all form av hjälp med rekryteringen från utomstående aktörer.



Anställning kan ske först efter att ett godtagbart registerutdrag från Polisens erhållits.



Haninge kommun ser mångfald som en styrka och välkomnar alla sökande. Vår kompetensbaserade rekryteringsmetodik syftar till att fokusera på individens kompetens och därigenom även motverka diskriminering.

Show more

Title: Data Engineer – Azure Databricks | Solna | 100%
Description:
Company Description

ARVKonsult AB is a trusted partner in digital transformation, delivering secure, scalable, and high-performing digital solutions. Specializing in Citrix, Microsoft AVD, Nutanix, and Public Cloud technologies, the company enhances digital workplaces for productivity, resilience, and security. In addition to IT consulting services provided by in-house employees, ARVKonsult AB collaborates with experienced subcontractors for client projects. With a strong emphasis on cybersecurity and value-driven delivery, ARVKonsult AB supports organizations in achieving long-term business success. As a proud authorized reseller partner with industry leaders like Nutanix, Rubrik, and Ivanti, ARVKonsult AB consistently drives impactful results.




Vi söker en Data Engineer till ett nytt team med fokus på att maximera värdet av data genom att bygga robusta datapipelines och användarvänliga dataprodukter på Azure Databricks. Du kommer att arbeta nära verksamheten, analytiker och data scientists för att utveckla lösningar inom rapportering, analys och AI samt bidra till etablering av arbetssätt enligt Data Mesh-principer.

Krav:

Erfarenhet av Azure Databricks eller liknande molnplattform.
God förståelse för datamodellering, granularitet och historikhantering.
Stark kommunikativ förmåga och erfarenhet av tvärfunktionellt samarbete.

Meriterande:

DevOps, CI/CD och Data Governance.
Erfarenhet av överlämning av lösningar till andra team.
Erfarenhet av analys inom kundbeteende, lönsamhet, risk eller operativ effektivitet.

📍 Solna | ⏰ 100% | 🚀 Start ASAP | 📅 Till 2026-12-31

🌍 Språk: Svenska & Engelska

Title: IT Support Intern
Description:
As an IT Support Intern, you will support the day-to-day IT operations at our office in Stockholm. You will assist the IT team in handling incoming requests, learning how to prioritize tasks, and contributing to improving and automating basic IT processes.

You will support both the IT team and, where needed, the global Group IT team on projects and security-related initiatives. This role offers hands-on experience in a fast-paced IT environment with opportunities to learn across multiple systems and technologies.

You will report to the IT Manager in Hamburg and be part of the Hamburg IT team. The role is primarily onsite.

YOUR MISSION


Assist in resolving IT support incidents and service requests, including basic troubleshooting of hardware, software, and network issues
Support basic network administration tasks, such as coordinating with vendors and assisting with troubleshooting local network issues
Help with user lifecycle management (onboarding and offboarding of users and devices)
Support asset management tasks, including maintaining inventory records and preparing equipment for new joiners
Assist in software and access provisioning for SaaS applications under guidance
Help with equipment ordering, inventory tracking, and vendor coordination
Communicate clearly and effectively with internal teams and employees
Support global IT operations in collaboration with international teams


YOUR BACKSTORY


Interest or background in IT (studies, training, or first experience in a similar environment)
Enrolled as a Student in (Computer Science, Information Technology / IT Management , Software Engineering )
Basic familiarity with Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android devices
Fluent in English; Swedish is a plus
Strong teamwork and communication skills
Positive attitude and willingness to learn


Stillfront is a global gaming company. We develop a wide range of digital games that attract millions of players each month. From well-established franchises like Supremacy, BIG, and Jawaker to niche games, we span multiple genres, including strategy, simulation, RPG, action, and casual and mash-up games.

We believe gaming can be a force for good, and we want to create a gaming universe that is digital, affordable, equal, and sustainable. To achieve this, we focus on developing games that are all about having a rewarding hobby, a great social experience, or a strategic challenge.

Stillfront was founded in 2010, and since then, we’ve grown rapidly. Our professionals thrive in an organization that embodies the spirit of entrepreneurship, and we’re proud to connect and empower game teams around the world.

Our HQ is in Stockholm, Sweden, but our game development is done by teams and studios all over the world. Our main markets are the US, Japan, MENA, Germany, and the UK. We’re proud that our company shares are listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm Large Cap.

Title: General Ledger Assistant
Description:
As a General Ledger Assistant at Embark you will be handling accounting in our financial system Visma.Net. In addition to accounting, the role will include other related tasks within the finance and payroll area such as, monthly closing, reconciliations, expense reporting as well as payroll administration. Your role also requires the ability to occasionally provide backup support for other team members as well as for Accounts Payable.

You will work in the finance department together with four colleagues. To enjoy the role you should be ambitious, accurate, good at taking initiative, structured and have good collaborative ability. The role includes many and close contact areas for users and employees; it is therefore important that you are comfortable communicating in English in both speech and writing.

Example of responsibilities


Accounting
Monthly reconciliations
Month end closing
Internal reporting within the Nexon group
Expense Report system Skovik
Payroll administration
Audit preparation



It would be awesome if you have


A degree in a Finance related subject and/or at least 2 years of experience in a similar role
Previous experience working in an international company is considered meritorious
Experience from working in an environment where you need to take own initiatives
Excellent attention to details, with a high level of accuracy
A mind-set for planning and prioritizing, with a focus on delivery
Professional English and communication skills, and an ability to communicate clearly and effectively



This is an 80% role (32 hours per week), with working days and schedule agreed upon together. There may be an opportunity for the position to expand to 100% over time, depending on business needs. This is a hybrid role, and we are only considering candidates already residing in Stockholm, Sweden.

At Embark we offer competitive salaries, passionate colleagues to share knowledge with and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.

Be yourself at Embark and make games while doing so. Please apply with confidence. We can’t wait to hear from you (in English)!

Title: Python Support Consultant
Description:
Role: Consultant - Python - Lead Production Support Engineer

Location: Stockholm, Sweden

Duration: Permanent




Job Description

Today, the corporate landscape is dynamic and the world ahead is full of possibilities! None of the amazing things we do at client would be possible without an equally amazing culture, the environment where ideas can flourish and where you are empowered to move forward as far as your ideas will take you.




At client, we assure that your career will never stand still, we will inspire you to build what’s next and we will navigate further together. Our journey of learnability, values and trusted relationships with our clients continue to be the cornerstones of our organization and these values are upheld only because of our people.




Your role

In the role of Lead Production Support Engineer with focus on Python-based applications, you will be responsible for proactive identification of issues in production environment leveraging observability tools with the aim of reducing MTTD and MTTR. You will also provide regular support/guidance to project teams on complex coding, issue resolution and execution.




You will collaborate with some of the best talent in the industry to create and implement innovative high-quality solutions. You will be part of a learning culture, where teamwork and collaboration are encouraged, excellence is rewarded, and diversity is respected and valued.




Responsibilities

Interfacing with technical and business stakeholders – internal and external (Managers / Application owners, Support Analysts, Infrastructure Team, Developers and technology specialists).
Managing and maintaining production environment.
Change management and application deployments.
Restore a normal service operation as quickly as possible and minimize the impact on business operations, thus ensuring that the best possible levels of service quality and availability are maintained.
Coordinate all activities required to lead incident triage in compliance with SLAs and OLAs.
Facilitate the outage calls and ensure that all the required resources are engaged to work a Major Incident.
Preparing internal reports on Major incidents and leading their review with Executive Management.
SLA & KPI reporting
Onsite – Offshore coordination
Available to be on-call and support production issues.

Required

Primary skill: Design, develop, test, and maintain Python-based applications.
Experience with Python frameworks such as Django, Flask, or FastAPI.
Write clean, efficient, and reusable code.
Troubleshoot, debug, and optimize applications
Application support and maintenance experience (tier 2 and tier 3), critical incidents, Monitoring & observability tools (APM, logs, alerts)
Incident management & problem management
Change management and production deployments
Batch job management & production
Dashboarding, automation and simplification
Application transition and steady state support
SLA & KPI management
SOX Compliance activities

  • Onsite – Offshore coordination
  • Available to be on-call and support production issues.

Required

  • Primary skill: Design, develop, test, and maintain Python-based applications.
  • Experience with Python frameworks such as Django, Flask, or FastAPI.
  • Write clean, efficient, and reusable code.
  • Troubleshoot, debug, and optimize applications
  • Application support and maintenance experience (tier 2 and tier 3), critical incidents, Monitoring & observability tools (APM, logs, alerts)
  • Incident management & problem management
  • Change management and production deployments
  • Batch job management & production
  • Dashboarding, automation and simplification
  • Application transition and steady state support
  • SLA & KPI management
  • SOX Compliance activities

Title: Data & Analytics Engineer
Description:
Why Join Us?

At Tobii Dynavox, we empower people with disabilities to do what they once did or never thought possible. We call this mission Power to Be You. Our assistive communication technology helps our customers express themselves, connect with the world, and pursue independence, whether through everyday activities like ordering food or extraordinary feats like running a company. Working at Tobii Dynavox, you’ll become part of a team that spans the globe, with offices in the US, Sweden, France, the UK, China, and beyond.





To learn more about what we make possible, meet some of our customers or take a look at some of our solutions




.
About the Ro

leAs a Data & Analytics Engineer at Dynavox Group, you’ll step into a broad and dynamic role where you will empower the entire organization with high-quality data and insights across all business areas. Your mission is to join us on our journey to develop the way we transform data into knowledge and knowledge into action, enabling data-driven decisions that move Tobii Dynavox forwar




d.
The Business Intelligence and Analytics team is responsible for the BI data estate including operating and developing an enterprise data warehouse, front end reports and semantic models as well as to continuously adapt to a changing catalogue of source systems and business requiremen




ts.
Please note: This is a hybrid role based in Stockholm, Sweden. Occasional international travel (10–20%) may be required and will be communicated in adva




nce.
In this role you

will:Work to improve and streamline BI data warehouse maintenance, enhancements and daily operat
ions.Translate business needs into technical solutions through hands-on development around architecture, coding and integration to contribute to a scalable, and stable BI architec
ture.Build and optimize data models, semantic layers, and ETL pipel
ines.Be part of the BI data warehouse maintenance, enhancement and daily operat
ions.Help develop capabilities within Azure technologies such as AI, Fabric, Event Hubs, Stream Analytics and Synapse pipel
ines.Work across the full data value chain from integrating data sources using connectivity tools and APIs, to transforming and aligning data, and delivering insights via reports, dashboards, and analytical mo
dels.Contribute to the improvement of Power BI dashboards and analytics solutions using the Microsoft BI s
tack.Collaborate with colleagues to support global data initiatives and participate in projects focused on customer usage data and event-based analy




tics.
What We’re Looki

ng ForAs we continue to strengthen our data landscape, the need for event-driven analytics, and ability to handle larger and more complex data sets is growing. We are therefore looking for a Data & Analytics Engineer with a strong engineering mindset, interest in business and passion for




data.
If having a key hands-on role driving our organization forward to be more data driven, modern and scalable applying modern data techniques excites you and sounds like a challenge you’d thrive in, we’d love to hear fr




om you!
We’re looking for a detail-oriented and analytical profession

al with:Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Engineering (Industrial or Mechanical) or similar fields with skills in statistics and re
porting.Experience in BI roles such as BI Specialist, Analyst, Consultant, or Data E
ngineer.Coding skills in SQL, Python/PySpark with interest in business processes and curiosity to explore programming languages or platforms as our solutions develop. Experience with TimeXtender or similar orchestration tools is
a plus.Familiarity with traditional database management, Azure event-based technologies (Event Hubs, Stream Analytics, etc.) and Microsoft Fabric or modern lake house concepts is
a plus.Communication, collaboration and interpersonal skills to work closely with external and internal stakeholders across various levels and fu
nctions.Proficiency in both spoken and written communication in English. Proficiency in Swedish is




valued.
Our

Values: At Tobii Dynavox, our mission guides what we do, and our values guide us in how we do it. Across the organization, we are committed to being Collaborative, Considerate, Curious and Courageous. We build a trusting environment where every team member prioritizes our customers with empathy and insight. Bold ideas and learning lead to impactful solutions. Driven by curiosity, we continuously challenge the status quo to create meaningful, customer-focused solutions for our c




ustomers.
What

We Offer: At Tobii Dynavox, we believe in empowering individuals – including our employees - to reach their full potential. Here’s what makes

us unique:Purpose-Driven Work: Join a company that transforms lives by giving a voice to those with communication challenges. Every day, your work makes a meaningful and concre
te impact.“Yes, and...” Flexibility: Build a rewarding career AND enjoy time with loved ones. We offer flexible work options so you don’t have to choose between personal and professio
nal goals.Growth and Development: Whether you’re advancing your skills or growing your career, we invest in your future with training, learning opportunities, and internal gro
wth paths.Inclusive and Supportive Culture: Work in a collaborative, caring environment where diversity and individuality are valued. You’ll feel connected to both your team and our global
community.A Global Leader with Heart: Be part of an innovative, forward-thinking company that combines experience and cutting-edge solutions with a mission to cha
nge lives.

Title: Man söker personliga assistenter – Hammarby sjöstad
Description:
Ansök senast: 30 juni 2026
Stockholm
Arbete på plats


Ansök nu


Ansök senast: 30 juni 2026
Stockholm
Arbete på plats


Arbetsuppgifter

Jag är en man som söker engagerade personliga assistenter som vill arbeta nära mig i min vardag. Tjänsten passar dig som har ett genuint intresse för människor och gärna erfarenhet av kvalificerad omvårdnad.

Arbetet innebär att ge stöd i vardagens alla moment, med fokus på trygg, lugn och professionell omvårdnad. Har du erfarenhet av ALS eller liknande vårdinsatser är det meriterande.

Det finns även möjlighet att ta ett utökat ansvar som arbetsledare/bemanningsansvarig, där du är med och planerar, leder och samordnar assistansen. Erfarenhet av arbetsledarroll är därför ett plus.

Jag Söker Dig Som


är lugn, lyhörd och empatisk
är tålmodig och trivs i en stillsam miljö
är i god fysisk form
gärna har erfarenhet av personlig assistans och/eller kvalificerad omvårdnad


Övrigt

Rekryteringen sker tillsammans med ansvarig representant, och första intervju hålls digitalt.

Välkommen med din ansökan!

Om rekryteringsprocessen: Vi tar löpande kontakt med intressanta kandidater och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdagen. Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess genomför vi alltid en bakgrundskontroll via Fortcheck som skickas ut till dig som kandidat via mejl och sms. Bakgrundskontrollen sker helt på dina villkor - du utför själv kontrollen och får ta del av resultatet. Du väljer sedan huruvida du vill skicka vidare informationen till oss eller avbryta.

Om JAG som arbetsgivare: Vi har kollektivavtal med Kommunal och erbjuder alla anställda betald introduktion, fortbildning och friskvårdsbidrag. Här kan du läsa om alla förmåner du erbjuds som personlig assistent hos oss.

Som anställd i JAG gör du skillnad varje dag. Välkommen du också!

Vill du veta mer om yrket personlig assistent?

Läs mer om oss på JAG.SE

Några av våra medarbetare

Victoria

Personlig assistent

"Har du en drivkraft att vilja hjälpa andra så kan det här arbetet passa dig. Du bestämmer över ditt liv, tänk att du får hjälpa den du assisterar att få bestämma över sitt!"

Klara

Personlig assistent

"Varje dag vaknar man upp och vet att man gör någonting meningsfullt. Nånting som är så stort. En större motivation än det går ju inte att hitta."

Simon

Personlig assistent

"Det bästa med jobbet är att man får lära känna andra på ett annat sätt. Jag har lärt mig tålamod. Att ta saker i egen takt har sina fördelar. Man blir lugn och behöver inte stressa fram."

Emma

Personlig assistent

"Jag har förändrats väldigt mycket som person av att jobba som assistent. Det är ett väldigt viktigt jobb. Jag ser världen på ett lite annat sätt."

Vi har riktigt bra förmåner


Friskvårdsbidrag
Samarbeten med bland annat gym och hotellkedjor


Om företaget

Vi är Sveriges största privata assistansanordnare som drivs utan vinstsyfte. Assistansen är särskilt utformad för personer med flera stora funktionsnedsättningar där en är den intellektuella förmågan. Organisationen har en stark ideologisk förankring som bottnar i alla människors lika värde. Namnet JAG valdes för att symbolisera varje assistansanvändares rätt att vara huvudperson i sitt liv, oavsett funktionsnedsättning. Vi finns i hela Sverige och har systerbolag i Norge och Finland.

Välkommen till oss du också!

2022Vi får höga betyg i medarbetarnöjdhet. 50+ enligt eNPS, i nivå med Apple och Sony. (På en skala från -100 till +100).

2022Fler än 4000 personliga assistenter arbetar hos oss.

2022Det finns stora chanser att hitta jobb där du bor - assistansanvändarna i JAG bor från Gällivare i norr till Ystad i söder.

2022Vi tillämpar individuell lönesättning.

2022Vi är den största assistansanordnaren i Sverige som drivs utan vinstsyfte.

2022JAG var med och drev igenom assistansreformen 1994.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Vi har nu ett nytt behov och söker därför en konsult och kollega med nedan kvalifikationer. Tjänsten är en del av vår konsultverksamhet vilket innebär att du blir anställd av oss och arbetar hos kund alt. interna projekt och åtaganden.

Din profil (Skall krav)


Minst 5 års arbetslivserfarenhet i rollen som Data Engineer, med erfarenhet av att utveckla organisationsövergripande data- och analysplattformar
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av utveckling av dataflöden, datamodeller eller analyslösningar i Microsoft Fabric och/eller Microsoft Azure Synapse.
Minst 3 års erfarenhet av in- och utgående dataintegrationer inom data- och analysplattformar i molnmiljö.
Minst 5 års erfarenhet av ETL/ELT, varav minst 2 år i Microsofts molnmiljö.
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av utveckling i Apache Spark Notebooks.
Minst 3 års arbetslivserfarenhet av dimensionsmodellering.
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av utveckling, konfiguration och förvaltning av CI/CD-pipelines i moderna utvecklingsmiljöer.
Minst 3 års dokumenterad erfarenhet av aktivt mentorskap och tekniskt stöd till mer juniora utvecklare, exempelvis genom kodgranskning, kompetensöverföring, utbildning eller coachning i team


Meriterande


Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av semantisk modellering i Power BI eller Azure Analysis Services.
Minst 1 tidigare uppdrag med utveckling i Data Lakehouse arkitektur.
Minst 2 års dokumenterad arbetslivserfarenhet av arbete med strömmande dataintag.


Sista ansökningdatum: 15-06-2026

Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!

🚀 Spännande uppdrag hos våra många kunder. Vi fokuserar på långsiktiga uppdrag, vilket ger dig möjlighet att skapa värde för kunden samtidigt som du utvecklas själv.

🌟 En trygg månadslön – tillsammans bestämmer vi en grundlön baserad på dina uppdrag.

💼 Tjänstepension och stora möjligheter till löneväxling.

🌴 Semester - Önskar du mer än 30 dagar semester, kanske 60 dagar?

🏥 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring, olycksfallsförsäkring och livförsäkring för ökad trygghet.

📚 Kompetensutveckling – du får själv sätta din budget och välja vilka utbildningar du vill delta i.

🕑 Livskvalitet - Har du kanske svårt att få ihop pusslet i vardagen och önskar arbeta lite mindre och ändå kunna tjäna lika mycket om inte mer?


Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande, med hänsyn till höga arbetstrycket är det extra viktigt med en kort motivering för tjänsten som förklarar hur man möter kraven.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Join EQT as a Data Engineer in the Client Tech Value Stream, where you will build and scale our modern data platform. You will work across the full data lifecycle, enabling reliable, high-quality data products that support decision-making across the business.

About The Client Tech Value Stream Team

The Client Tech Value Stream focuses on delivering technology and data solutions that support client, investor, and fund-related processes. The team works closely with engineering, product, CRM, and business stakeholders to build scalable platforms that enable insights and operational efficiency across EQT.

About The Role

You will play a key role in designing, building, and operating EQT’s cloud-based data platform. You will work across ingestion, transformation, and data modeling to ensure high-quality, reliable, and accessible data.


Design, build, and operate scalable data pipelines across multiple data sources.
Ingest data from APIs, external platforms, Fivetran connectors, and cloud storage.
Develop and maintain data models using dbt on Snowflake.
Structure data around core business entities such as clients, investors, funds, and transactions.
Enable analytics and reporting in tools such as Power BI, Salesforce CRM Analytics, Tableau and our AI stack.
Monitor and troubleshoot pipelines to ensure reliability and performance.
Collaborate with stakeholders across engineering, analytics, CRM, and business teams.
Contribute to platform improvements and modernization of legacy pipelines.
Support infrastructure and access management using GitHub, Terraform, and Snowflake.


About You

You are an experienced Data Engineer who enjoys working across the full data stack and collaborating with diverse teams. You are comfortable working in a modern cloud environment and translating business needs into scalable data solutions. You are curious about AI and new technology, and can translate both human and machine needs into code.

Essential skills and experience:


Experience building data pipelines and working with cloud data platforms.

Nice to have:


Experience with GCP and Fivetran.

What We Offer

You will be part of a collaborative and forward-looking team where data is central to how EQT creates value. We offer opportunities to work with modern technologies, contribute to impactful projects, and grow your skills in a global environment.

Inclusion at EQT

Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing & engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact. Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness; We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About SCRL




SCRL is a mobile-first design platform built for social media and used by millions of creators worldwide.

We're a team based in central Stockholm, working closely across functions with a shared commitment to quality, clarity, and craft.




The role




This is a role with real impact and plenty of room to grow. You'll join the data team and work on the infrastructure that powers SCRL's analytics, forecasting, and AI-driven product features — from event ingestion all the way to the data services our apps rely on.

We're open to a range of experience levels. If you're earlier in your career, you'll learn alongside experienced data and engineering teammates and grow into broader ownership of the systems you build. If you come in with more experience, you'll have the opportunity to shape the direction of our data infrastructure and raise the bar for what we build next.

Either way, you'll work on real systems with real users — and you'll have the freedom to do the best work of your career.




What you will do




Data pipelines & infrastructure

Contribute to our ETL across the stack, helping make it easier to use, more reliable, and clearer when something goes wrong.
Build and maintain ingestion pipelines from the services we rely on (RevenueCat, Firebase Analytics, attribution providers, ad networks, and others) via API and event streaming.
Work in our BigQuery warehouse — schema design, Terraform-managed infrastructure, partitioning, and query optimization for a dataset that serves millions of users.
Develop and maintain GCP Cloud Functions and Cloud Run services for event processing, transformations, and scheduled jobs.
Help evolve our internal Python framework for shared models, utilities, and pipeline components used across the data codebase.




Analytics & modeling support

Bring data scientists' and analysts' models into production so they can be consumed reliably by the apps, backend services, and internal tools.
Build and maintain the data services that power product features such as template recommendations and personalization.
Support subscription analytics infrastructure: cohorting, LTV, ROAS, retention, and forecasting pipelines across iOS and Android.
Develop internal libraries and shared SQL/Python primitives for app metrics calculation, used by the broader team.




Data quality, observability & craft

Help implement monitoring, alerting, and data quality checks so we catch issues before stakeholders do.
Contribute to lineage and documentation that makes the warehouse navigable for everyone who uses it.
Support GDPR-compliant data handling, including consent propagation, retention policies, and deletion across systems.
Help maintain consistency of event schemas and metric definitions across web and mobile platforms.
Be a contributor to — and over time a champion of — data culture across the company.




You should have




Technical foundations

Languages: Solid proficiency in Python and SQL. Comfort with TypeScript/NodeJS or similar for service work is a plus, not a requirement.
Warehousing: Hands-on experience with cloud data warehouses, ideally BigQuery — or strong fundamentals you're ready to transfer.
Pipelines: Some experience building or contributing to production data pipelines, from ingestion through transformation to serving.
Cloud infrastructure: Exposure to GCP (or AWS/Azure) is a strong plus. Familiarity with infrastructure as code (e.g. Terraform) is welcome but can be learned on the job.




Mindset & approach

Curiosity and care for craft: You take pride in the quality of what you ship and want to keep getting better at it.
Data pipelines owner mindset: You're interested in fully managing data flows from ingestion to processing, even if you haven't done it end-to-end yet.
Quality and pragmatism: You can work at a healthy pace without compromising the things that matter — clarity, reliability, and correctness.
Can-do attitude: Self-motivated problem solver with an eye for detail and an internal drive for excellence.




Product & business understanding

Subscription apps: Experience with or strong interest in subscription-based app businesses and freemium models.
Cross-platform thinking: Interest in the complexities of unifying data across web, iOS, and Android.
Analytics-driven approach: Comfortable working closely with analysts and data scientists, and wanting the data layer you build to support the insights the business needs.




Collaboration & communication

Team player: You work closely and generously with analysts, data scientists, backend, and mobile engineers.
Clear communication: You can explain what you're working on in a way that makes sense to teammates who don't share your technical background.




We offer




High-impact role: You'll work on the data platform that powers analytics, forecasting, and AI features for millions of users.
Room to grow: Take ownership of systems from early on, with support from experienced teammates and the space to develop into broader scope over time.
Startup environment: Fast-paced decision making with direct impact on product direction.
Strong team: Work alongside experienced data and engineering professionals in central Stockholm.
Competitive package: Market-rate salary with opportunities for professional development.
Creative culture: Be part of a profitable company that values creativity and user experience.
Show more

Title: Finance & BI Analytics Specialist, Quant
Description:
We are seeking a proactive and analytically driven Finance & BI Analytics Specialist to join our team in Stockholm. In this role, you will play a key part in bridging finance and business intelligence, turning data into actionable insights that support strategic decision-making.

Quant is a global leader in industrial maintenance services, supporting customers across multiple industries worldwide. With over 30 years of experience, we improve safety, productivity, and asset performance at more than 400 facilities globally. Headquartered in Stockholm, Quant operates across approximately 50 sites worldwide and employs around 1,700 people.

What you will do:

Business Intelligence & Reporting

Oversee the maintenance, development, and improvement of Power BI reports and our BI platform, including financial dashboards, KPI tracking, and operational reports.
Participate in sprint planning, prioritize the backlog, define user stories, and collaborate with stakeholders across Finance, Operations, and IT.
Handle maintenance and support tasks, ensuring high availability and data integrity in all reports.
Optimize existing products and solutions, adhering to established processes, routines, and instructions.
Act as a test lead and solution architect when needed, planning and performing quality-enhancing initiatives to ensure the product fulfills its purpose over its lifecycle.

Finance & Business Partnering

Collaborate with the Finance function to develop and maintain financial reports, including P&L overviews, cost tracking, and budget vs. actuals dashboards in Power BI.
Apply a basic understanding of financial concepts — such as P&L structure, KPIs, and cost drivers — to translate business requirements into meaningful BI solutions.
Support Finance and Operations stakeholders in interpreting data and turning reports into actionable insights.
Bridge the gap between technical BI capabilities and business needs, ensuring financial reporting is consistent, reliable, and aligned with management requirements.

Collaboration & Stakeholder Management

Work with business and process owners on new demands, from analyzing needs to delivering changes.
Steer, guide, and engage in constructive dialogue with vendors and software suppliers.
Propose and approve changes to the technical environment.
Ensure proper system, product, and solution documentation is updated.
Address support-related tasks and solve problems in your product area.

Who are you?

You hold a post-secondary degree and have 2–3 years of experience specializing in Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, and data transformation. You are skilled in building reports and dashboards in Power BI and assisting business users — including Finance and Operations teams — in meeting their reporting requirements. You understand ETL flows and can perform initial troubleshooting. You are also proficient in Fabric, Databricks, and PySpark.

Importantly, you bring a basic understanding of financial reporting and business economics. You are familiar with concepts such as P&L structure, key financial KPIs, cost categorization, and budget/forecast reporting. You do not need to be a controller or accountant, but you are comfortable working alongside Finance stakeholders and translating their data needs into BI solutions.

Personal Qualities

Customer-focused with a systematic and structured approach to problem-solving.
Committed to quality, with high standards in your deliverables.
Proactive and capable of leading projects independently or participating actively in a team.
A collaborative team player who thrives working across functions — Finance, IT, and Operations.
Comfortable operating in a global matrix organization.

Language Requirements

We are a global organization, and fluency in both written and spoken English and Swedish is required. Knowledge of Spanish or Portuguese is advantageous.

What We Offer:

Join a forward-looking organization and seize the opportunity to advance your career. You will be part of a supportive and collaborative team with the mission to leverage data as an asset and drive digitalization and financial insight in the maintenance industry.

A role at the intersection of technology and finance — rare and highly valued.
Work alongside experienced Finance and Operations teams in a truly international environment.
Hybrid work model with flexibility in your work location, with presence on dedicated office days in Stockholm.
The opportunity to grow within a company that is a global leader in industrial maintenance.

Does this sound like the right role for you? Submit your application via our online application system on our careers page.

Title: Anbudsansvarig
Description:
Linjemontage är ett modernt företag inom elkraftbranschen som utför entreprenadarbeten inom området kraftförsörjningsanläggningar ifrån 0,4 kV till 400 kV. Det kan vara allt från komplexa leveranser av kompletta transformatorstationer, leveranser av kraftledningar till utbyte av delar av system som ex.v fjärrkontroll, reläskydd m.m. Våra kunder är bl.a elnätsägare, industrier, kommunala och statliga förvaltningar. Linjemontage har kontor och verksamhet över hela Sverige samt i Norge och en filial i Kroatien. Vi är drygt 400 anställda. Sedan 2019 är Linjemontage en del av Kalpataru Projects International Ltd

Vill du arbeta i en affärsnära roll där du är med och möjliggör framtidens energiinfrastruktur? Linjemontage fortsätter växa och vi söker nu en anbudsansvarig som vill bidra till att vinna och utveckla våra projekt inom affärsområde - Transmission.

Arbetsuppgifter I rollen ansvarar du för att samordna och driva anbudsprocessen från start till inlämning. Du arbetar med att strukturera upp arbetet, följa upp tidplaner och säkerställa att alla delar av anbudet uppfyller ställda krav.

Du sammanställer och kvalitetssäkrar anbudsdokumentation, koordinerar interna resurser och har en löpande dialog med både tekniska specialister och andra funktioner inom organisationen. I arbetet ingår även att stötta i framtagning av underlag, kalkyler och övrigt innehåll som krävs för att skapa konkurrenskraftiga anbud.

Rollen innebär många kontaktytor och du blir en viktig länk mellan teknik och affär, där din förmåga att skapa struktur och helhet är avgörande.

Kvalifikationer Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av anbudsarbete, projektadministration eller en liknande koordinerande roll. Du har ett strukturerat arbetssätt, är noggrann och trivs med att arbeta mot deadlines i en miljö där flera processer pågår parallellt.

Har du kunskap inom elkraft, energi eller infrastruktur ser vi det som mycket meriterande, då det underlättar förståelsen för våra projekt. Erfarenhet från entreprenadbranschen eller arbete med offentliga upphandlingar (LOU) är också en fördel. Erfarenhet av internationella inköp är meriterande och ses som en tillgång i rollen.

Du har god kommunikativ förmåga och uttrycker dig obehindrat på svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift.

Arbetsplats Järfälla

Välkommen med din ansökan!

Och du! Följ oss gärna på Linkedin

Vi sköter rekryteringen själva Vi uppskattar alla erbjudanden om hjälp med rekrytering, men vi har valt att hantera denna process internt. Vi ber därför vänligen att inga externa rekryteringsföretag kontaktar oss angående denna annons.

Tack för förståelsen!
